centers.english: the body of a person or animal

examples.english: By the time I got home my body ached all over and I knew I was getting the flu.
examples.chinese: 回到家时我全身上下都觉得疼，我知道自己得了流感。

examples.english: If you don't start taking care of your body, you're going to have a heart attack one of these days.
examples.chinese: 假如你还不注意身体，早晚会得心脏病。

examples.english: There are over 1000 muscles in the human body.
examples.chinese: 人体有一千多块肌肉。

centers.english: the shape, size, or strength of a person's body
centers.chinese: 人体的体形、大小或力量

examples.english: Calvin was not happy with his body, no matter how much he exercised.
examples.chinese: 卡尔文无论做多少运动仍然对自己的体形不满意。

examples.english: Jane Fonda has an amazing body for a woman of her age.
examples.chinese: 作为这个年纪的妇女，简·方达的体形令人惊叹。

examples.english: You're exactly the right build for a rugby player -- you've got good strong broad shoulders.
examples.chinese: 你这种体格很适合打橄榄球——你的肩膀又宽又结实。

examples.english: He looks rather like me -- we both have the same build.
examples.chinese: 他看上去很像我—我们俩的身材都一样。

examples.english: The man the police are looking for is about thirty years old, blond, and of medium build.
examples.chinese: 警方正在寻找的男子约三十岁，金黄色头发，中等身材。

examples.english: When she was younger, Margaret was good-looking and charming, and had a lovely figure.
examples.chinese: 玛格丽特年轻时漂亮迷人，体态优美。

examples.english: Susie wore a close-fitting black dress which made the most of her figure.
examples.chinese: 苏茜穿一条黑色紧身连衣裙，把她的身材衬托得恰到好处。

examples.english: She eats enormous meals but still manages to keep her figure.
examples.chinese: 她吃得非常多，但身材仍然保持得很好。

examples.english: Exercise and a sensible diet will help you get your figure back after having a baby.
examples.chinese: 生育之后，体育锻炼加上适当的饮食会帮助你恢复体形。

examples.english: William was tall and handsome and had a slim, muscular physique.
examples.chinese: 威廉个子很高，相貌英俊，身材瘦削而结实。

examples.english: Brad had a superb physique and the looks of a young Marlon Brando.
examples.chinese: 布拉德体格强健，相貌就像年轻时的马龙·白兰度。

examples.english: The cancer may have spread to other parts of her body.
examples.chinese: 癌细胞也许已扩散到她身体的其他部位。

examples.english: More heat is lost through the head than through any other part of the body.
examples.chinese: 从头部散失的热量比从身体其他任何部位散失的热量都要多。

examples.english: Each exercise is designed to build up muscles in a different part of your body.
examples.chinese: 每项运动都是为了锻炼身体不同部位的肌肉而设计的。

examples.english: Mr Price's long body and short arms and legs gave him a rather strange appearance.
examples.chinese: 普赖斯先生躯干部分很长，手脚很短，这使他看上去有点怪。

examples.english: The black widow spider has red-orange markings on its body.
examples.chinese: 黑寡妇蜘蛛身上有橘红色的花纹。

examples.english: Baby monkeys cling to their mothers’ bodies until they are old enough to start climbing by themselves.
examples.chinese: 小猴子紧紧抱住母猴的身体，直到它们长大可以独立爬树为止。

examples.english: Kevin liked to walk around the house in nothing but a pair of jeans, showing off his muscular torso.
examples.chinese: 凯文喜欢只穿一条牛仔裤在屋里走来走去，以炫耀自己强健的躯体。

examples.english: The search led to the discovery of a headless torso in the woods.
examples.chinese: 经过搜索，在树林里发现了一具无头尸体。

examples.english: When babies are born they have very little control over their limbs.
examples.chinese: 婴儿刚出生时，很难控制四肢的活动。

examples.english: Hundreds of children have lost limbs after stepping on mines.
examples.chinese: 数以百计的儿童因踩上地雷而失去了手脚。

examples.english: The calf stood up slowly, with trembling limbs and took its first, uncertain steps.
examples.chinese: 小牛犊四足颤抖着缓缓站起来，摇摇晃晃地迈出了第一步。

examples.english: The liver is an extremely complex organ.
examples.chinese: 肝是极为复杂的器官。

examples.english: This diagram shows the position of the main organs of speech.
examples.chinese: 这张示意图显示了主要发音器官的位置。

examples.english: Her vital organs are intact and she has a good chance of recovery.
examples.chinese: 她的重要器官未受损伤，所以复原的机会很大。

centers.english: the body of a dead person or animal

examples.english: Police found the body of a young boy in Epping Forest last night.
examples.chinese: 昨天夜里，警方在埃平森林里发现了一具男孩的尸体。

examples.english: The woman fell to her knees beside her son's body and began crying and wailing.
examples.chinese: 那个妇人跪倒在儿子的尸体旁号啕大哭起来。

examples.english: The bodies of the two soldiers were buried with full military honors at Arlington National Cemetery.
examples.chinese: 两名士兵的遗体以隆重的军葬礼下葬在阿灵顿国家公墓。

examples.english: The first time I ever saw a dead body was at my grandfather's funeral.
examples.chinese: 我第一次看到尸体是在祖父的葬礼上。

examples.english: Thieves are digging up corpses in order to steal jewellery and gold teeth.
examples.chinese: 窃贼们把尸体挖出来以盗取珠宝和金牙。

examples.english: The streets were filled with the stench of decaying corpses.
examples.chinese: 街上弥漫着腐烂的尸体发出的臭味。

examples.english: They found the remains of a young woman under the floor boards.
examples.chinese: 他们在地板下面发现了一名年轻女子的尸骸。

examples.english: These rocks contain the fossilised remains of extinct animals.
examples.chinese: 这些岩石中有绝种动物遗骸的化石。

examples.english: The architect's remains are interred in St Paul's cathedral.
examples.chinese: 那位建筑师的遗体被埋在圣保罗大教堂里。

examples.english: They burned Gandhi's body and scattered the ashes on the waters of the Jumna river.
examples.chinese: 他们火化了甘地的遗体，把骨灰撒在朱木拿河中。

examples.english: Kay kept her father's ashes in an urn on the mantelpiece.
examples.chinese: 凯把父亲的骨灰存在骨灰瓮内，放在壁炉架上。

examples.english: Your son appears to be in good physical health.
examples.chinese: 你儿子看上去身体不错。

examples.english: She has suffered terrible physical as well as emotional abuse for over 12 years.
examples.chinese: 她在肉体上和精神上受到严重折磨达12年以上。

examples.english: A lot of British people avoid physical contact with strangers.
examples.chinese: 许多英国人避免与陌生人有身体上的接触。

examples.english: Man's primary needs are physical -- food, drink and sleep.
examples.chinese: 人类最基本的需求是生理上的需求——吃、喝、睡。

examples.english: Nearly three quarters of the women surveyed said they were satisfied with their physical fitness.
examples.chinese: 受访妇女中有接近3/4的人表示对自己的身体健康很满意。

examples.english: At the end of the race she was completely exhausted, both mentally and physically.
examples.chinese: 比赛结束时，她在精神上和身体上都疲惫不堪。

examples.english: At the age of 70 he's still physically very active.
examples.chinese: 他70岁时身体仍充满活力。

examples.english: Albert agreed to turn over samples of his hair and bodily fluids to the court.
examples.chinese: 艾伯特同意把自己的头发及体液样本交给法庭。

examples.english: Parretti had a lengthy criminal record that included fraud and conspiracy to commit bodily harm.
examples.chinese: 帕雷蒂有冗长的犯罪记录，包括欺诈和同谋伤人。

examples.english: The villagers have to go down to the lake to perform most of their bodily functions.
examples.chinese: 村民不得不跑到湖边去解决大部分的个人卫生问题。

examples.english: I think Sue's a really nice person.
examples.chinese: 我认为休确实是个好人。

examples.english: He's the only person I know who can speak Chinese.
examples.chinese: 他是我认识的唯一会说中文的人。

examples.english: There were over 200 people at the meeting.
examples.chinese: 与会的有200多人。

examples.english: The streets were suddenly full of people.
examples.chinese: 街上一下子满是人。

examples.english: Someone phoned you but I didn't get their name.
examples.chinese: 有人打电话找你，但我没听清他的姓名。

examples.english: What would you do if somebody tried to rob you in the street?
examples.chinese: 要是有人在街上抢你东西，你会怎么做？

examples.english: Can't you get someone else to clean the kitchen for you?
examples.chinese: 你难道不能让别人来替你打扫厨房吗？

examples.english: The drug had never before been tested on a human being.
examples.chinese: 这种药从未在人类身上试验过。

examples.english: Computers have replaced humans in many factories.
examples.chinese: 在许多工厂，电脑替代了人类。

examples.english: It is the responsibility of each individual within the class to make sure they have the correct books.
examples.chinese: 把书本备齐是班里每个人自己的责任。

examples.english: The decision to have an operation should be up to the individual involved.
examples.chinese: 是否要动手术应该由当事人自己来决定。

examples.english: A couple of suspicious-looking characters were standing outside the house.
examples.chinese: 有几个看起来很可疑的人正站在房子外。

examples.english: Beneath his brash, noisy exterior was a much shrewder and lonelier character than he admitted.
examples.chinese: 他表面上傲慢、吵闹，内里却要比他承认的更精明、更孤独。

examples.english: People are getting very worried about rising crime.
examples.chinese: 民众十分担忧不断增加的犯罪案例。

examples.english: I don't want people to feel sorry for me.
examples.chinese: 我不愿人们替我难过。

examples.english: Most people hate writing essays, but I quite like it.
examples.chinese: 大多数人讨厌写文章，但是我相当喜欢。

examples.english: Don't you like ice-cream? I thought everyone liked it!
examples.chinese: 你不喜欢吃冰淇淋吗？我原以为每个人都喜欢呢！

examples.english: Everybody has the right to a good education.
examples.chinese: 人人都有受良好教育的权利。

examples.english: Everyone knows that smoking is bad for you.
examples.chinese: 人人都知道吸烟有害。

examples.english: Folks around here have been pretty angry about the governor's actions.
examples.chinese: 这里的人对州长的做法相当愤怒。

examples.english: Some folks think the schools are better now than they were twenty years ago.
examples.chinese: 有些人觉得现在的学校比20年前的好。

examples.english: Pollution is threatening the future of the human race.
examples.chinese: 污染正威胁着人类的未来。

examples.english: The entire human race could be wiped out by nuclear war.
examples.chinese: 核战争可以毁灭全人类。

expressions.text: a member of the human race

examples.english: Until then, no member of the human race had ever been able to make a map of the whole world.
examples.chinese: 那之前，没有一个人能绘制出世界地图。

examples.english: The Americans exploded the first nuclear weapon in the history of mankind.
examples.chinese: 美国人引爆了人类历史上第一件核武器。

examples.english: Travelling into space was a great advance for mankind.
examples.chinese: 太空航行对人类来说是一大进步。

examples.english: In the interests of humankind we must stop destroying our planet.
examples.chinese: 为了全人类的利益，我们必须停止破坏地球。

examples.english: Jericho is the oldest continuously inhabited city known to man.
examples.chinese: 杰里科是人类已知最古老的一直有人居住的城市。

examples.english: The grandeur of the mountains is a constant reminder of man's insignificance.
examples.chinese: 雄伟的山脉一直提醒着我们人类的渺小。

examples.english: The Dutch reclamation of their land is a classic case of man's struggle against nature.
examples.chinese: 荷兰人围海造田是人类与大自然抗争的一个经典例子。

examples.english: 30% of humanity live in conditions of terrible poverty.
examples.chinese: 30%的人生活在极为贫困的条件下。

examples.english: The General was accused of committing crimes against humanity.
examples.chinese: 那位将军被控反人类罪。

examples.english: The castle is open to the public during the summer.
examples.chinese: 那座城堡夏季对公众开放。

examples.english: The public ought to know how the money from taxes is being spent.
examples.chinese: 公众应当知道税收的去向。

examples.english: Some of these politicians never meet ordinary members of the public.
examples.chinese: 这些政治家中有些从未会见过普通民众。

examples.english: Tickets will become available to the general public in June.
examples.chinese: 门票将于6月向公众发售。

examples.english: Public attitudes to homosexuality are gradually changing.
examples.chinese: 公众对于同性恋的态度渐渐在转变。

examples.english: The plan cannot succeed without public support.
examples.chinese: 没有大众的支持，这项计划无法成功。

examples.english: Islamic society
examples.chinese: 伊斯兰教社会

examples.english: The judge described Smith as ‘a danger to society’.
examples.chinese: 法官形容史密斯是“社会的祸害”。

examples.english: We want our students to become useful and responsible members of society.
examples.chinese: 我们希望自己的学生成为社会中有用、负责的人。

examples.english: The young folk need to have a place where they can go in the evenings.
examples.chinese: 年轻人需要一个晚上能去的地方。

examples.english: Stella's ambition is to get a job working with old folk.
examples.chinese: 斯特拉追求的是同老年人打交道的工作。

examples.english: His parents were hard-working country folk.
examples.chinese: 他的父母是勤恳的农村人。

examples.english: Rising unemployment led to even more social problems.
examples.chinese: 不断上升的失业率导致了更严重的社会问题。

examples.english: social changes that brought women even greater freedom
examples.chinese: 为女性带来更大自由的社会变革

centers.english: all the people in a particular area, city, country etc
centers.chinese: 住在某个地区、城市、国家等里的所有的人

examples.english: In 1966 the population of Lima was about two million.
examples.chinese: 1966年，利马的人口约为200万。

expressions.text: a population of five million/twenty million etc

examples.english: New Jersey has a population of around 7.6 million.
examples.chinese: 新泽西州约有760万人口。

examples.english: 30% of the male population suffers from heart disease.
examples.chinese: 30%的男人患有心脏病。

examples.english: In our study, significantly more miners complained of weight loss than the general population.
examples.chinese: 我们的研究发现，与大众相比，更多矿工抱怨体重减轻。

examples.english: Reagan's views were shared by a majority of the American people.
examples.chinese: 大多数美国人同意里根的看法。

examples.english: the awful sufferings of the people of Sarajevo
examples.chinese: 萨拉热窝居民承受的可怕的痛苦

examples.english: The French are famous for their love of good food.
examples.chinese: 法国人以热爱美食而著称。

examples.english: The murder has shocked the local community.
examples.chinese: 凶案令当地社区震惊。

examples.english: New York's Jewish community
examples.chinese: 纽约的犹太人社区

examples.english: For most New Yorkers, life will never be the same again.
examples.chinese: 对于大多数纽约人来说，生活永远不会再像以前那样了。

examples.english: The Milanese people from Milan elected a new mayor yesterday.
examples.chinese: 昨天，米兰市民选出了新的市长。

examples.english: It was a wonderful story -- the characters were so convincing.
examples.chinese: 那是个精彩的故事—人物形象都很可信。

examples.english: The interesting thing about the play is the conflict between the two main characters.
examples.chinese: 这部戏的有趣之处在于两个主要角色之间的冲突。

examples.english: Indiana Jones is the hero of the film.
examples.chinese: 印第安纳·琼斯是这部电影的男主人公。

examples.english: Hamlet is Shakespeare's most famous tragic hero.
examples.chinese: 哈姆雷特是莎士比亚笔下最著名的悲剧男主人公。

examples.english: The heroine of her latest novel is a middle-class English woman.
examples.chinese: 她新小说中的女主人公是一位中产阶级的英国女性。

centers.english: relating to people, not animals or machines
centers.chinese: 与人而不是动物或机器有关的

examples.english: the effects of pollution on the human and animal population
examples.chinese: 污染对人口和动物数量的影响

examples.english: Bacteria cannot be seen with the human eye.
examples.chinese: 细菌无法用人类肉眼看见。

examples.english: There were only two pieces of bread per person.
examples.chinese: 每人只能分到两片面包。

examples.english: You can get a decent meal for less than £20 per person.
examples.chinese: 每人不用20英镑就可以吃到相当不错的一餐了。

examples.english: We paid £5 a head for our Christmas dinner.
examples.chinese: 我们每人付了五英镑吃圣诞晚餐。

examples.english: Guests were paying $800 a head for luxury hotel accommodation.
examples.chinese: 豪华的酒店住宿每位客人要支付800美元。

examples.english: Among the largest consumers of energy per capita is the United States.
examples.chinese: 美国的人均能源消耗量居世界前列。

examples.english: The average per capita income has decreased over the past five years.
examples.chinese: 过去五年里人均收入下降了。

examples.english: In Europe the per capita supply of trained medical staff has increased dramatically.
examples.chinese: 在欧洲，经过培训的医务人员人均量大幅度地增加了。

examples.english: No-one was home, so I left a note.
examples.chinese: 没人在家，于是我留了一张字条。

examples.english: He explained what had happened but nobody believed him.
examples.chinese: 他向人们解释发生了什么事，但没有人相信他。

expressions.text: no one at all/nobody at all

examples.english: Nobody had supported him, nobody at all.
examples.chinese: 没有人支持他，一个都没有。

examples.english: It was strange. There wasn't a soul in the street.
examples.chinese: 真奇怪，街上空无一人。

expressions.text: not a soul to be seen/not a soul in sight

examples.english: Steve looked in every room, but there was no sound and not a soul to be seen.
examples.chinese: 史蒂夫查看了每个房间，但什么声音也没听见，一个人也没看见。

centers.english: words for describing someone who is fat

examples.english: Peter was a fat little boy.
examples.chinese: 彼得是个胖胖的小男孩。

examples.english: I'm getting too fat for my clothes.
examples.chinese: 我长得太胖，衣服都穿不下了。

examples.english: After thirty years, Teddy looked just the same, only a little fatter.
examples.chinese: 30年以后特迪看上去还是这个样，只是胖了些。

examples.english: Most fat people simply eat too much by normal standards.
examples.chinese: 大多数胖子都吃得比正常标准多得多。

examples.english: Though she weighed only six stone, she thought she was fat.
examples.chinese: 她体重才6英石，可她觉得自己很胖。

examples.english: The doctor said I was slightly overweight and that I needed more exercise.
examples.chinese: 医生说我有点儿超重了，需要多锻炼。

examples.english: The majority of overweight people who diet tend to gain the weight back within a few years.
examples.chinese: 大多数节食的超重者几年后会回复原状。

examples.english: My mother is about 50 pounds overweight.
examples.chinese: 我母亲大约超重50磅。

examples.english: People who are grossly overweight are more likely to suffer from high blood pressure.
examples.chinese: 严重超重的人更有可能患高血压。

examples.english: Even though she's big, Rosemary is an attractive woman.
examples.chinese: 虽然块头挺大，罗斯玛丽是个吸引人的女子。

examples.english: It's often difficult for large people to find fashionable clothes that fit them.
examples.chinese: 块头大的人常常不容易买到合身的时装。

examples.english: Mrs. Medlock is a large woman, with a very red face and bright black eyes.
examples.chinese: 梅德洛克夫人是个大个子女人，长着红扑扑的脸和一双明亮的黑眼睛。

examples.english: He runs quite fast for a big man.
examples.chinese: 以他这么大的块头跑得也真够快的了。

examples.english: Stevie is a plump healthy-looking child.
examples.chinese: 史蒂维是个胖胖的、外表健康的小孩。

examples.english: Frieda's mother was a plump, cheerful woman, quick with a laugh.
examples.chinese: 弗丽达的母亲是个胖胖的、性情愉快的、爱笑的女人。

examples.english: He's a little on the plump side, but nevertheless quite handsome.
examples.chinese: 他有点胖，但十分英俊。

examples.english: The baby was pink and chubby.
examples.chinese: 这婴儿长得粉嘟嘟、胖乎乎的。

examples.english: Who's that chubby little girl with the dark hair?
examples.chinese: 那个长得胖乎乎的黑发小女孩是谁？

examples.english: The bishop was a dignified, portly man, with thinning white hair.
examples.chinese: 主教是个头发稀疏花白、很有威严的大腹便便的人。

examples.english: the portly figure of General von Hindenburg
examples.chinese: 冯·兴登堡将军那大腹便便的体形

examples.english: Both of Myra's sons were hefty, energetic boys.
examples.chinese: 迈拉的两个儿子都高大健壮，精力充沛。

examples.english: The mechanic was a big hefty man who could lift up the front of a truck with his bare hands.
examples.chinese: 这位技工是个彪形大汉，徒手就能抬起卡车头。

examples.english: The second prisoner was a fat-faced beefy fellow who smelled of sweat.
examples.chinese: 第二名囚犯是个胖脸大块头家伙，身上一股汗味。

examples.english: Amanda and Tina were both beefy, sporty types.
examples.chinese: 阿曼达和蒂娜两个都属于肥实的运动型。

examples.english: The farmer was a big, burly man with a red face.
examples.chinese: 这位农夫是个大个子，身材魁梧，脸色红红。

examples.english: Hey! Fatso! Pull up a couple of chairs!
examples.chinese: 喂，胖子！拉几把椅子过来！

examples.english: Glenda is not just fat, she's obese.
examples.chinese: 格伦达不单是胖了，她是胖得臃肿。

examples.english: a special summer camp for obese children and teens
examples.chinese: 为肥胖的少年儿童举办的特殊夏令营

examples.english: He may not be clinically obese, but he certainly needs to lose a lot of weight.
examples.chinese: 按医学标准他可能算不上肥胖，但肯定需要好好减减肥了。

examples.english: Obesity can lead to heart disorders and other health problems.
examples.chinese: 肥胖症会引起心脏疾病以及其他健康问题。

examples.english: The program is aimed at reducing obesity among women.
examples.chinese: 该计划旨在减少女性肥胖症患者的人数。

examples.english: Clara was a plain, dumpy woman, several years older than her husband.
examples.chinese: 克拉拉是个相貌平平的矮胖女人，年龄比她丈夫大几岁。

examples.english: In his youth, William had been a dumpy little boy who ate too many sweets.
examples.chinese: 威廉小时候是个胖胖的男孩，糖果吃得很多。

examples.english: I think this skirt makes me look dumpy.
examples.chinese: 我觉得穿这条裙子让我显得矮胖。

examples.english: The banker was a tubby, jolly-looking man.
examples.chinese: 银行家是位胖墩墩、乐呵呵的男子。

examples.english: He's getting a bit tubby -- too much of his wife's cooking I expect.
examples.chinese: 他有点长圆了—我想是吃了太多他妻子做的菜吧。

examples.english: A stout woman in a tweed coat was standing outside the door.
examples.chinese: 一个身穿粗花呢外套的矮胖女人站在门外。

examples.english: Amy was now stout and matronly, the mother of three children.
examples.chinese: 埃米现在身材矮胖有点中年发福，已是三个孩子的母亲了。

centers.english: a part of the body that is fat

examples.english: Sally hates going swimming -- she thinks her legs are too fat.
examples.chinese: 萨莉不喜欢去游泳，她觉得自己的腿太粗了。

examples.english: The colonel rubbed his fat stomach, reaching for something from the table to put into his mouth.
examples.chinese: 上校摸了摸自己的胖肚子，伸手拿起桌上的食物放进嘴里。

examples.english: My stomach's much fatter than yours.
examples.chinese: 我的肚子比你的大多了。

examples.english: The doctor's wife had a plump face and a small mouth.
examples.chinese: 医生的妻子长着胖乎乎的脸和樱桃小口。

examples.english: Paula had silver bracelets on her plump arms.
examples.chinese: 葆拉丰腴的手臂上戴着银手镯。

examples.english: The baby reached out with its chubby little fingers.
examples.chinese: 婴儿伸出胖乎乎的小手指来。

examples.english: Mark's cheeks were slightly chubby.
examples.chinese: 马克的脸蛋有点胖乎乎的。

examples.english: His pudgy little fingers were covered in chocolate.
examples.chinese: 他胖鼓鼓的小手指上全是巧克力。

examples.english: Elsie had a large body and a podgy face.
examples.chinese: 埃尔茜块头挺大，却长着一个胖鼓鼓的小脸蛋。

examples.english: Her body was getting old and flabby.
examples.chinese: 她身体开始变老变松弛了。

examples.english: John's flabby white thighs wobbled as he walked across the beach.
examples.chinese: 约翰走过海滩时，他那两条白皙松弛的大腿在一颤一颤的。

examples.english: Joanna's full red lips were fixed in an inviting smile.
examples.chinese: 乔安娜丰满的红唇上挂着迷人的微笑。

examples.english: A black necklace hung down over her full breasts.
examples.chinese: 一条黑色项链挂在她丰满的胸脯上。

examples.english: You're getting a paunch, did you know that?
examples.chinese: 你有大肚子了，自己知道吗?

examples.english: Merv lay back in his chair with his hands crossed above his paunch, listening to the radio.
examples.chinese: 梅尔韦双手搭在大肚子上躺在椅子里听收音机。

examples.english: Wally crossed the mirrored lobby, sucking in his paunch as he caught sight of himself.
examples.chinese: 沃利穿过镶有镜子的通道，看到镜子里的自己时就往里收了收大肚子。

examples.english: Larry was just a regular guy: short, with a pot belly and moustache.
examples.chinese: 拉里就是个普普通通的家伙：个子矮矮的，挺着大肚子，留着一撮小胡子。

examples.english: You really should do something about that pot belly of yours.
examples.chinese: 你的肚子那么大，真该采取点行动了。

examples.english: Our history teacher was a balding, pot-bellied man of about 50.
examples.chinese: 我们的历史老师是男的，开始秃顶，肚子很大，约50岁。

examples.english: pot-bellied politicians, puffing cigars
examples.chinese: 大腹便便的政客们，抽着雪茄烟

examples.english: His beer gut was popping the buttons on his shirt.
examples.chinese: 他的啤酒肚把衬衫上的纽扣都绷开了。

examples.english: At only seventeen, Lonny already had the beginnings of a beer gut.
examples.chinese: 朗尼才17岁的时候就开始长啤酒肚了。

examples.english: You should go on a diet -- look at that spare tyre!
examples.chinese: 你应该控制饮食—看你那肉肚子！

examples.english: A lot of people start to get middle-age spread once they pass 30.
examples.chinese: 许多人过了30岁就开始发福了。

examples.english: John's put on a lot of weight recently, hasn't he?
examples.chinese: 约翰最近胖了好多，是不是？

examples.english: I put on several pounds while I was on holiday, so now I'm on a strict diet.
examples.chinese: 我休假的时候重了好几磅，所以现在我正严格控制饮食。

examples.english: When I was young, I could eat all I wanted without gaining weight.
examples.chinese: 年轻时，我可以任意吃爱吃的东西而不增加体重。

examples.english: Ben's gained at least five kilos since he was born.
examples.chinese: 本出生以后重了至少5公斤。

examples.english: Gerry was terribly thin when he came out of hospital, but he's filled out a lot since then.
examples.chinese: 格里出院时瘦得可怕，不过后来胖了好多。

examples.english: Sue looks much healthier now and her face has started to fill out.
examples.chinese: 休现在看上去健康多了，脸上也开始长肉了。

examples.english: The women in his family were always trying to fatten him up.
examples.chinese: 他家里的女人们一直都想让他长胖些。

examples.english: I shouldn't have any more of this cake. It's way too fattening.
examples.chinese: 我决不能再吃那种蛋糕了，吃这很容易长胖。

examples.english: Grill the fish instead of frying it. It's less fattening that way.
examples.chinese: 把鱼烤来吃，不要油炸。那样就不容易发胖。

examples.english: Avoid fattening desserts -- eat a piece of fruit instead.
examples.chinese: 不要吃让人发胖的甜食，改为吃一片水果。

examples.english: The farmers are fattening up their cattle and getting them ready for market.
examples.chinese: 农民们正在把牛养肥准备上市。

centers.english: to take air into your lungs and send it out again
centers.chinese: 将空气吸入肺中再呼出

examples.english: The air was so smoky it was difficult to breathe.
examples.chinese: 空气中烟雾弥漫，令人感到呼吸困难。

examples.english: The boy was unconscious, but he was still breathing.
examples.chinese: 那男孩失去了知觉，但仍有呼吸。

examples.english: People nowadays are becoming more and more concerned about the quality of the air they breathe.
examples.chinese: 人们现在愈来愈关心他们呼吸的空气的质量。

examples.english: I want you to breathe deeply and relax.
examples.chinese: 我要你深呼吸并放松。

centers.english: to take air, smoke etc into your lungs

examples.english: Every time I breathe in I get a pain in the left side of my chest.
examples.chinese: 我每次吸气时，左胸部就疼。

examples.english: I put my handkerchief over my nose to avoid breathing in the smoke.
examples.chinese: 我用手帕捂住鼻子以免吸入浓烟。

examples.english: They stood on the cliff breathing in the fresh sea air.
examples.chinese: 他们站在悬崖上吸着新鲜的海洋空气。

examples.english: Sherman stopped, took a breath, and opened the door.
examples.chinese: 舍曼停下来吸了口气，然后打开门。

examples.english: It was so quiet that I was afraid to take a breath.
examples.chinese: 安静得我连气也不敢喘。

examples.english: Every time you took a breath of that foul air you could feel it burning your lungs.
examples.chinese: 你每吸一口那里的污浊空气，都会感到它在烧炙你的肺部。

examples.english: Taking a deep breath she dived into the cool water.
examples.chinese: 她深深地吸了口气便跳入冰凉的水中。

examples.english: It is dangerous to inhale the fumes produced by these chemicals.
examples.chinese: 吸入这些化学品产生的气体是很危险的。

examples.english: Every time he inhaled, his lungs made an awful wheezing sound.
examples.chinese: 他每吸一口气，肺部就会发出可怕的呼哧声。

examples.english: Stella lit up a cigarette and inhaled deeply.
examples.chinese: 斯特拉点起一支烟，深深吸了一口。

centers.english: to send air, smoke etc out of your lungs

examples.english: The doctor told her to breathe out slowly.
examples.chinese: 医生叫她缓缓地呼气。

examples.english: Ballet dancers are taught to breathe in before they leap, and to breathe out after they land.
examples.chinese: 芭蕾舞演员受过训练，知道跃起前要吸气，落地后则呼气。

examples.english: Hold your breath for 5 seconds, then exhale slowly.
examples.chinese: 屏气5秒钟，然后慢慢呼出。

examples.english: She took a long pull on her cigarette, exhaled and coughed loudly.
examples.chinese: 她抽了一大口烟，吐烟时大声咳嗽起来。

examples.english: I put the balloon to my lips and blew as hard as I could.
examples.chinese: 我把气球放到嘴唇边使劲地吹气。

examples.english: He blew smoke rings across the table.
examples.chinese: 他吐出一个个烟圈，从桌上飘过。

examples.english: ‘This coffee's too hot to drink.’ ‘Blow on it -- that'll cool it down.’
examples.chinese: “这杯咖啡太烫了，没法喝。”“把它吹一下一会凉下来的。

examples.english: The disease in his lungs made breathing very painful.
examples.chinese: 肺部的病使他呼吸时很疼痛。

examples.english: Breathing became more difficult as we got higher up the mountain.
examples.chinese: 我们往山上走得越高，呼吸就越困难。

examples.english: Deep breathing is good for relaxing your mind and your body.
examples.chinese: 深呼吸对你放松身心有好处。

examples.english: When I picked up the phone all I heard was heavy breathing.
examples.chinese: 我拿起电话，只听见沉重的呼吸声。

examples.english: It was so cold they could see their breath.
examples.chinese: 天冷得很，他们都能看到自己呼出的热气。

examples.english: I could feel the horse's breath on the back of my neck.
examples.chinese: 我能感觉马在我脖子后的呼吸。

examples.english: How long can you hold your breath underwater?
examples.chinese: 你能在水中屏气多长时间？

examples.english: His teeth were rotten and he had bad breath.
examples.chinese: 他的牙齿蛀了，嘴里有口臭。

examples.english: Stop sniffing! Use your handkerchief.
examples.chinese: 别再哧哧地抽鼻子了！用手帕揩吧。

examples.english: ‘I'm sorry I got so upset,’ she sniffed.
examples.chinese: “对不起，我太难过了。”她抽泣着说道。

examples.english: The dog raised its nose in the air, sniffed, and then started to follow the scent.
examples.chinese: 狗抬起鼻子嗅了嗅，然后开始追踪这种气味。

examples.english: His mother gave a sniff and asked if he had been smoking in his bedroom.
examples.chinese: 他母亲闻了闻，然后问他是否在卧室里吸过烟。

examples.english: Frank sighed deeply and stared out of the window.
examples.chinese: 弗兰克深深叹了口气，凝望着窗外。

examples.english: ‘That's life, I suppose,’ she sighed.
examples.chinese: “我想那就是人生了，”她叹息道。

examples.english: Sighing wearily, she began her routine of getting ready for bed.
examples.chinese: 她疲惫地叹着气，又像往常一样铺床去了。

examples.english: ‘Oh no!’ he said with a sigh, ‘Not again!’
examples.chinese: “噢，算了吧！”他叹了口气说，“不要再来了！”

examples.english: Irene closed the door behind her and breathed a big sigh of relief.
examples.chinese: 艾琳把门关上后大大地舒了一口气。

examples.english: I gasped when I heard how much the ring had cost.
examples.chinese: 我听到那枚戒指的价钱时，倒抽了一口气。

examples.english: The crowd gasped as the plane burst into flames.
examples.chinese: 飞机突然起火燃烧时，人们倒吸一口气。

examples.english: One of the boys hit him in the face, and he gasped with pain.
examples.chinese: 一个男孩打在他脸上，他痛得吸了一口气。

examples.english: There were gasps of astonishment from the audience.
examples.chinese: 观众惊讶得倒抽了一口气。

examples.english: My husband snores so loudly that I find it difficult to get to sleep.
examples.chinese: 我丈夫的鼾声太响，我很难入睡。

examples.english: If you snore, it's better not to sleep on your back.
examples.chinese: 你如果打鼾就不要仰着睡。

examples.english: Isn't there something you can do about your snoring?
examples.chinese: 就没有什么办法让你睡觉时不打鼾吗？

examples.english: My sister snorts when she laughs.
examples.chinese: 我妹妹笑时会发出哼哼声。

examples.english: The horse lowered its head, and snorted at them.
examples.chinese: 马低下头，朝着他们喷鼻息。

examples.english: From the other side of the library came a loud unmistakable snort -- it was the professor.
examples.chinese: 图书馆那头有个人大声地哼了一声，不会弄错的—那是教授。

examples.english: When I wake up in the morning I'm often very short of breath.
examples.chinese: 我早晨醒来时常觉得气喘得厉害。

examples.english: She got short of breath very easily because she was so overweight.
examples.chinese: 她太胖了，所以动不动就喘不过气来。

examples.english: The fog irritated his lungs and made him short of breath.
examples.chinese: 大雾令他的肺部不适，使他呼吸困难。

examples.english: She sounded a little breathless, as if she had been running.
examples.chinese: 她气喘吁吁，仿佛是一路跑过来的。

examples.english: We were all a little out of breath when we got on the train.
examples.chinese: 我们上火车后都有些喘气。

examples.english: Can we have a rest? -- I'm a bit out of breath.
examples.chinese: 我们歇一歇好吗？—我有点喘不过气来了。

examples.english: The poor lady, judging by her breathlessness and flushed cheeks, was exhausted.
examples.chinese: 那位可怜的女士气喘吁吁、满脸通红，看样子已经累坏了。

examples.english: The hill was very steep and they were all gasping by the time they got to the top.
examples.chinese: 那座小山非常陡峭，他们到达山顶时，都已经上气不接下气了。

examples.english: ‘Do you think you can walk?’ I asked. ‘I'll try,’ he gasped.
examples.chinese: “你觉得自己能走吗？”我问道。“试试看，”他喘着气说。

examples.english: His mother was coughing and gasping for breath.
examples.chinese: 他母亲咳得很厉害，不停地喘气。

examples.english: You could see her puffing as she carried the heavy washing basket.
examples.chinese: 她提着洗衣篮时看得出她气喘吁吁。

examples.english: ‘Sorry I'm so late Maxie,’ he said, puffing breathlessly.
examples.chinese: “马克西，很抱歉我迟到了。”他上气不接下气地说道。

examples.english: Matt was still panting after his run.
examples.chinese: 马特跑完之后仍气喘吁吁。

examples.english: When I reached the top of the stairs I was puffing and panting like an old steam engine.
examples.chinese: 我爬到楼梯顶时喘得就像一台老式的蒸汽发动机。

examples.english: A strange brown dog suddenly jumped all over him, panting, its tongue out.
examples.chinese: 一只奇怪的棕色狗突然向他扑来扑去，吐着舌头喘着气。

expressions.text: be stuffed up/ be bunged up

examples.english: She doesn't want to go to school. She says her throat is sore and she's stuffed up.
examples.chinese: 她不愿上学去。她说喉咙痛，鼻子也塞住了。

examples.english: I couldn't sleep last night because I was all bunged up.
examples.chinese: 昨夜我无法入睡，因为鼻子全堵了。

examples.english: When she coughed she made a terrible wheezing sound.
examples.chinese: 她咳嗽时发出了可怕的气喘声。

examples.english: His asthma was acting up and he wheezed throughout the show.
examples.chinese: 他哮喘病发作起来，整个表演他都气喘不止。

centers.english: to breathe normally again after running, playing sport etc
centers.chinese: 跑步、运动等后呼吸恢复正常

expressions.text: get your breath back/catch your breath

examples.english: It took me a few minutes to get my breath back after climbing the stairs.
examples.chinese: 我爬上楼梯后过了好几分钟才缓过气来。

examples.english: Once you've got your breath back we can do a few more lengths of the pool.
examples.chinese: 等你缓过气以后，我们可以在游泳池里多游几个来回。

examples.english: It's so hot in here! I can't breathe!
examples.chinese: 这里太热了！我简直透不过气来！

examples.english: The worst thing about asthma is feeling that you can't breathe.
examples.chinese: 哮喘最让人头疼的就是感到气接不上来。

examples.english: Do something -- he's choking!
examples.chinese: 想想办法—他噎住了！

examples.english: At last I reached the shore and fell onto the sand, choking and spluttering.
examples.chinese: 我终于到了岸边，倒在沙滩上感到窒息，不断噗噗地吐气。

examples.english: The old king died after choking on a chicken bone.
examples.chinese: 年迈的国王被一根鸡骨头噎死了。

examples.english: Many of the birds had suffocated in their boxes.
examples.chinese: 许多鸟在盒子里窒息而死。

examples.english: It was very hot inside the car, and I felt as though I was suffocating.
examples.chinese: 车内热得要命，我感到自己快要闷死了。

examples.english: Glue-sniffing carries the risk of suffocation.
examples.chinese: 吸胶毒有使人窒息的危险。

centers.english: to make it impossible for someone to breathe

examples.english: Don't hold so tight, you're choking me.
examples.chinese: 别抓得这么紧，你快把我掐死了。

examples.english: I felt myself being choked by thick, yellow fumes.
examples.chinese: 我感到自己被黄色的浓烟呛得透不过气来。

examples.english: He grabbed her around the neck and choked her to death.
examples.chinese: 他抓住她的脖子，把她掐死了。

examples.english: They pushed a plastic bag over his head and almost suffocated him.
examples.chinese: 他们把一只塑料袋硬套在他头上，几乎把他闷死。

examples.english: They found her half-suffocated from the poisonous gases given off by the burning furniture.
examples.chinese: 他们发现她被起火家具散发出的毒气呛得半死。

examples.english: You're sweating. Why don't you take your jacket off?
examples.chinese: 你在出汗，为什么不把夹克衫脱了呢?

examples.english: The heat from the lights was making her sweat and her make-up started to run.
examples.chinese: 灯光热得她直流汗，脸上的妆都开始褪色了。

examples.english: Sweating profusely and gripping the lectern, Anderson began his speech.
examples.chinese: 安德森双手抓着讲台，大汗淋漓地开始了他的演讲。

examples.english: My God, it's hot in here - I'm sweating like a pig!
examples.chinese: 我的天哪，这儿很热—我浑身是汗！

examples.english: It was tough work. Within minutes we were all sweating buckets.
examples.chinese: 这工作很费力，没几分钟我们全都浑身冒汗了。

examples.english: She felt hot and awkward and started to perspire.
examples.chinese: 她很热，又感到很难堪，就开始流汗了。

examples.english: He was perspiring. It showed on his forehead.
examples.chinese: 他在流汗，额头上能看出来。

examples.english: James, who was perspiring profusely, took out a handkerchief and mopped his brow.
examples.chinese: 詹姆斯大汗淋漓，他拿出一块手帕擦了擦额头。

expressions.text: break out in a sweat/break into a sweat

examples.english: I was terrified. My hands were shaking, and I broke out in a sweat.
examples.chinese: 我害怕极了，双手发抖，浑身冒汗。

examples.english: ‘It's got nothing to do with me,’ she said, breaking into a sweat.
examples.chinese: “这事与我无关。”她说，出了一身的汗。

expressions.text: break out in a cold sweat/break into a cold sweat

examples.english: As the security guard approached, I broke into a cold sweat.
examples.chinese: 保安向我走来，我吓出一身冷汗。

examples.english: What on earth have you been doing? You're dripping with sweat!
examples.chinese: 你到底在干什么？弄得汗水直流的！

examples.english: Melissa was pouring with sweat, and her hair was stuck to her face.
examples.chinese: 梅丽莎汗水直淌，头发都粘到脸上了。

centers.english: the liquid that appears on your skin when you sweat

examples.english: He stopped working for a moment to wipe the sweat off his face.
examples.chinese: 他歇了一会，擦去脸上的汗水。

examples.english: I got sweat in my eyes and couldn't see.
examples.chinese: 我眼里都是汗水，看不见了。

examples.english: Beads of sweat appeared on her forehead and she trembled visibly.
examples.chinese: 她额头上冒出了一颗颗汗珠，身体抖得厉害。

examples.english: a deodorant that protects against perspiration all day long
examples.chinese: 全天有效的抑汗香体液

examples.english: To keep your body cool, wear cotton clothing that absorbs perspiration.
examples.chinese: 穿吸汗的棉布衣服可以保持身体凉爽。

examples.english: Beads of perspiration trickled down my back.
examples.chinese: 我汗流浃背。

examples.english: I'm all hot and sweaty. I think I'll take a shower.
examples.chinese: 我热得满身是汗，想洗个淋浴。

examples.english: He was a short fat guy with big sweaty hands.
examples.chinese: 他是一个又矮又胖的家伙，一双大手汗津津的。

examples.english: The equipment manager collected the sweaty uniforms and took them down to the hotel laundry.
examples.chinese: 设备经理把汗湿的制服收起来，送到宾馆的洗衣房里去。

expressions.text: be covered/drenched/soaked in sweat/be covered/drenched/soaked with sweat

examples.english: Ian came off the squash court covered in sweat.
examples.chinese: 伊恩从壁球场上下来，浑身是汗。

examples.english: I insist my car's have air conditioning - I hate arriving somewhere drenched with sweat.
examples.chinese: 我坚持我的车上要有空调—我讨厌到达一个地方时汗流浃背的样子。

centers.english: to damage paper, cloth, or clothing

examples.english: She unwrapped the present carefully, trying not to tear the paper.
examples.chinese: 她小心翼翼地打开礼品包装，尽量不把包装纸撕破。

examples.english: I had torn the knees of my jeans on the rough gravel.
examples.chinese: 我在粗糙的碎石上把牛仔裤的膝部划破了。

examples.english: She tore a page out of her diary and wrote her phone number on it.
examples.chinese: 她从日记本上撕下一页，把她的电话号码写在上面。

examples.english: The attendant tore off the parking ticket and handed it back.
examples.chinese: 管理员撕下一张违章停车罚款单交回。

examples.english: Peterson tore open the envelope.
examples.chinese: 彼德森撕开信封。

examples.english: I tore a hole in my jacket, climbing over the fence.
examples.chinese: 我爬过篱笆时把上衣挂了个洞。

examples.english: He took my ticket and tore it in half. ‘Row J, seats 8 and 9.’
examples.chinese: 他拿过我的票撕成两半。“J排，第8和第9座。”

examples.english: Stop pulling my dress! You'll rip it!
examples.chinese: 别拉我的裙子了！你会把它扯破的！

examples.english: You can see where the label has been ripped out.
examples.chinese: 你能看得出标签是从哪里撕掉的。

examples.english: I ripped my skirt on a broken chair.
examples.chinese: 我的裙子被一把破椅子扯破了。

examples.english: Beth excitedly ripped open the package.
examples.chinese: 贝丝兴奋地把包裹撕开。

examples.english: Damn! I've laddered my tights!
examples.chinese: 该死！我的裤袜抽丝了！

examples.english: He bent down and split his trousers.
examples.chinese: 他弯下身子时把裤子绷开了。

centers.english: to deliberately destroy something by tearing it into pieces
centers.chinese: 故意将某物撕成碎片将其损坏

examples.english: After Alan left, she tore up all his old letters.
examples.chinese: 艾伦离开后，她把他以前写来的信都撕碎了。

examples.english: Pamela tore the note up and threw it in the wastebasket.
examples.chinese: 帕梅拉把纸条撕碎，扔进废纸篓里。

examples.english: Use torn-up newspaper to line the bottom of the rabbit's cage.
examples.chinese: 用碎报纸垫在兔笼底下。

examples.english: Martine ripped up her essay and started again.
examples.chinese: 马蒂娜一把撕碎了她的文章，然后从头开始写。

examples.english: He'd get frustrated and throw his pencil down and rip his paper up.
examples.chinese: 他会变得很沮丧，然后把铅笔扔了，把纸也撕了。

examples.english: The superintendent gave his secretary some letters to shred.
examples.chinese: 主管把一些信件递给他的秘书，让她用碎纸机把它们销毁。

examples.english: Some photographs and important documents -- the only evidence available -- had been shredded.
examples.chinese: 一些照片和重要的文件——唯一能找到的证据—已经被碎纸机销毁了。

examples.english: Karen tore his photograph to shreds.
examples.chinese: 卡伦把他的照片撕得粉碎。

examples.english: He tore out the page and ripped it to shreds.
examples.chinese: 他撕下那一页，把它撕得粉碎。

examples.english: My jacket caught on a nail and tore.
examples.chinese: 我的外套在钉子上钩破了。

examples.english: Don't pull on the cloth, it will tear.
examples.chinese: 别把布猛拉，会撕破的。

examples.english: Be careful, the paper tears easily.
examples.chinese: 当心，这张纸很容易撕破。

examples.english: Tom heard his shorts rip as he climbed over the gate.
examples.chinese: 汤姆翻过大门时，听见自己的短裤被挂破了。

examples.english: My zipper was stuck, and the material around it ripped as I pulled on it.
examples.chinese: 我的拉链卡住了，我拉的时候周围的布料被撕破了。

examples.english: Hudson's coat had split right up the back.
examples.chinese: 赫德森的外套后背上撕破了。

examples.english: These shoes are so old the canvas had split.
examples.chinese: 这双鞋旧得帆布面都破了。

examples.english: He was wearing torn trousers and a ragged jacket.
examples.chinese: 他穿着破的裤子和破旧的外套。

examples.english: The pages of the book were torn and faded.
examples.chinese: 这本书的纸张已经破碎褪色了。

examples.english: If the cover of a book gets torn, the library sends it to be repaired.
examples.chinese: 如果书的封面被撕破，图书馆就会把它送去修复。

examples.english: The curtains hung in shreds and the carpet was worn thin.
examples.chinese: 挂着的窗帘已经破破烂烂，地毯也已被磨薄了。

examples.english: The clothes were handed down in the family, and by the time they reached the last child, they were in shreds.
examples.chinese: 这些衣服在家中一直往下传，到最后一个孩子手中时已经破烂了。

examples.english: He had on frayed jeans and an old white shirt.
examples.chinese: 他穿了磨损了的牛仔裤和旧的白衬衫。

examples.english: The jacket was a little frayed at the cuffs.
examples.chinese: 这件夹克衫的袖口有点磨损了。

examples.english: A man in tattered blue dungarees was busy in the garage.
examples.chinese: 一个穿着破旧的蓝色工装裤的男人正在汽车修理站里忙着。

examples.english: The old diary was yellowed and tattered.
examples.chinese: 这本旧日记本已发黄了，而且破旧不堪。

examples.english: The blanket she wore over her shoulders was ragged and filthy.
examples.chinese: 她披在肩上的毯子又破又脏。

examples.english: A man in ragged clothes was begging on the corner.
examples.chinese: 一名衣衫褴褛的男子在拐角处乞讨。

centers.english: a hole that is made when something is torn
centers.chinese: 某物被撕破时造成的洞眼

examples.english: How did you get that tear in your jacket ?
examples.chinese: 你夹克衫上的裂口是怎么回事?

examples.english: There's a small tear near the corner of the painting.
examples.chinese: 靠近画的一角处有个小破洞。

examples.english: The rips in the boat's old sails had been patched again and again.
examples.chinese: 这艘船旧帆上的裂口被补了又补。

examples.english: A rip in a repair worker's protective suit increases the risk of getting a shock as they work on the electricity lines.
examples.chinese: 修理工防护服上的裂缝使他们在修理电线时受到电击的风险增加了。

examples.english: There's a run in these pantyhose.
examples.chinese: 这双连裤袜上有个抽丝口。

examples.english: I got a ladder in my tights.
examples.chinese: 我的连裤袜上有个地方抽丝了。

examples.english: The driver was still conscious when the ambulance reached her.
examples.chinese: 救护车到达时，司机的神志还是清醒的。

examples.english: Frank was found lying beside the road, covered in blood but still conscious.
examples.chinese: 弗兰克被人发现躺在路边，浑身是血，但神志还清醒。

examples.english: The man was so drunk that he was barely conscious.
examples.chinese: 那个男人酩酊大醉，神志不清。

examples.english: They won't allow us to see her until the anesthesia has worn off and she's fully awake.
examples.chinese: 要等麻醉药性过去，她完全醒过来，他们才允许我们见她。

examples.english: Will I be awake by the time I get back to the ward?
examples.chinese: 我回到病房时会醒过来吗？

centers.english: to become conscious again after being unconscious
centers.chinese: 失去知觉后又清醒过来

examples.english: The doctors don't know when he'll regain consciousness.
examples.chinese: 医生们不知道他何时能恢复知觉。

examples.english: She died without regaining consciousness.
examples.chinese: 她没能恢复知觉便去世了。

examples.english: Sue was coming around, but she still felt dizzy.
examples.chinese: 她渐渐醒过来了，但仍觉得头晕。

examples.english: The police are waiting for him to come round so they can question him about the attack.
examples.chinese: 警方在等待他苏醒过来，以便能够问他有关袭击的情况。

examples.english: He came to a few minutes later, unable to remember anything about the accident.
examples.chinese: 几分钟过后他渐渐苏醒过来，完全记不起那场事故。

examples.english: Paramedics eventually brought the man around.
examples.chinese: 护理人员终于让那男子恢复了知觉。

examples.english: Elsie had fainted in the heat, and they were having difficulty bringing her round.
examples.chinese: 埃尔茜热得晕了过去，他们难以让她恢复知觉。

examples.english: There was a woman lying unconscious on the floor.
examples.chinese: 一个女人倒在地上失去了知觉。

examples.english: The unconscious man was carefully lifted onto a stretcher.
examples.chinese: 那名不省人事的男子被小心地抬上担架。

examples.english: A heavy concentration of the drug may produce dizziness or even unconsciousness.
examples.chinese: 这种药浓度高时可能引起头晕，甚至失去知觉。

examples.english: She managed to crawl into a shed, and then lapsed into unconsciousness.
examples.chinese: 她挣扎着爬进棚屋，然后就渐渐失去了知觉。

examples.english: Marina has been in a coma for the past four months.
examples.chinese: 玛丽娜在过去四个月内一直处于昏迷状态。

examples.english: Jo Columbo lay in a coma, paralyzed by bullet wounds.
examples.chinese: 乔·科伦坡由于身中数枪而瘫痪，处于昏迷状态。

examples.english: The other boxer was out cold.
examples.chinese: 另一个拳击手不省人事。

examples.english: When the realization hit her, she just sat there looking dazed.
examples.chinese: 她猛然清醒过来，就坐在那里，显得神志恍惚。

examples.english: I stumbled from the room dazed and confused, completely disoriented by what had just happened.
examples.chinese: 我从房间跌跌撞撞地走出来，恍惚而又疑惑，刚才的一切让我晕头转向。

examples.english: I remember looking down and seeing blood, and then I lost consciousness.
examples.chinese: 我记得自己低头看见了血，然后就不省人事了。

examples.english: After Atkins collapsed and lost consciousness at work, she was sent to the hospital for a scan.
examples.chinese: 阿特金斯在工作中晕倒后不省人事，被送到医院做扫描检查。

examples.english: One of the soldiers guarding the palace fainted in the heat.
examples.chinese: 其中一名守卫皇宫的士兵热得晕了过去。

examples.english: I need to go outside. I feel as if I'm going to faint.
examples.chinese: 我要出去一下，我觉得要晕过去了。

examples.english: I must have fainted, and when I came to I didn't know where I was.
examples.chinese: 我一定是晕倒了，醒来时不知道自己在哪儿。

examples.english: When I first smoked a cigarette, I almost passed out.
examples.chinese: 我第一次抽烟时差点没昏过去。

examples.english: I think the poor guy passed out. It looks like he's had a lot to drink.
examples.chinese: 我想那个可怜的家伙昏过去了，看样子他喝了不少。

examples.english: Her father's been having blackouts, and the doctor has ordered a brain scan.
examples.chinese: 她父亲老是晕倒，医生要求他做脑部扫描。

examples.english: A man grabbed her and put a piece of cloth to her face. That's the last thing she remembers before blacking out.
examples.chinese: 一名男子一把抓住她，往她脸上蒙了块布。那是她记得的最后一件事，接着便晕倒了。

examples.english: The patient slipped into unconsciousness at around 7 am.
examples.chinese: 病人上午7时左右逐渐失去了知觉。

examples.english: She managed to say a few words before falling into unconsciousness again.
examples.chinese: 她勉强说了几句话又渐渐地昏了过去。

examples.english: The patient slipped into a coma, and died two days later.
examples.chinese: 病人陷入昏迷状态，两天后死亡。

examples.english: After the accident she fell into a coma, and was on a life support machine for 6 months before regaining consciousness.
examples.chinese: 事故发生之后她陷入昏迷，依靠机器维持生命，六个月后才恢复了意识。

examples.english: Come quickly, one of the passengers has collapsed.
examples.chinese: 快来，有位乘客昏倒了。

examples.english: After half a dozen glasses of whisky he collapsed and could not be revived.
examples.chinese: 六杯威士忌酒下肚，他便昏倒了，然后就醒不过来了。

centers.english: to make someone unconscious by giving them a drug
centers.chinese: 用药物使某人失去知觉

examples.english: Once the patient was fully anesthetized, the surgeon made a small incision in his chest.
examples.chinese: 病人一旦完全麻醉后，外科医生就在他胸部切开一个小口。

examples.english: The doctor told me she was going to put me to sleep.
examples.chinese: 医生告诉我，她要给我施麻醉药让我失去知觉。

examples.english: I needed something to knock me out - it was the only way to escape from the pain.
examples.chinese: 我需要用点麻醉药，想要不痛这是唯一的方法。

centers.english: to make someone unconscious by hitting them

examples.english: There was a fight, and Mark was knocked unconscious.
examples.chinese: 他们打架了，马克被打得不省人事。

examples.english: I hit him on the jaw and knocked him out.
examples.chinese: 我一拳击在他下巴上，把他打晕了。

examples.english: Louis knocked out his opponent in the first round.
examples.chinese: 路易斯在第一回合把对手击昏了。

examples.english: The impact was so sudden and so fierce, it knocked me out cold.
examples.chinese: 这撞击又突然又猛烈，把我击昏了。

examples.english: Murphy was attacked by two men and beaten unconscious.
examples.chinese: 墨菲遭两名男子袭击，被打得不省人事。

examples.english: She was beaten senseless and left for dead.
examples.chinese: 她给打得失去知觉，然后被扔下等死。

examples.english: They had only intended to stun the guard, to give them time to escape.
examples.chinese: 他们只是想把守卫打晕，来争取时间逃跑。

examples.english: Gamekeepers use special darts to stun the rhinos.
examples.chinese: 猎场看守人用特殊的飞标使犀牛失去知觉。

examples.english: Charlotte was sleeping and her mother didn't want to wake her.
examples.chinese: 夏洛特在睡觉，她母亲不想叫醒她。

examples.english: If my snoring is that bad, I'll go down and sleep on the sofa.
examples.chinese: 如果我打鼾这么厉害的话，我就下楼到沙发上去睡。

examples.english: I had slept only a few hours, but I had to get up early.
examples.chinese: 我只睡了几个小时，但不得不早起。

examples.english: I'm so tired, I could sleep for a week.
examples.chinese: 我累得能睡上一个星期。

examples.english: Is the baby sleeping all night now?
examples.chinese: 宝宝现在能整夜睡觉吗？

examples.english: I didn't sleep very well last night, so I couldn't concentrate on the exam.
examples.chinese: 我昨晚没有睡好，所以考试不能集中注意力。

examples.english: I watched the sleeping child, the gentle rise and fall of her breast.
examples.chinese: 我看着睡梦中的孩子，看着她胸口轻轻地一起一伏。

examples.english: Eight hours’ sleep a night is enough for most people.
examples.chinese: 大多数人一晚上睡八小时就足够了。

examples.english: Depression can be caused simply by a lack of sleep.
examples.chinese: 单是缺少睡眠就可能会引起抑郁症。

expressions.text: get some/a lot/not much etc sleep

examples.english: I don't suppose you got much sleep last night.
examples.chinese: 我猜你昨天晚上睡得不多。

examples.english: Katie sometimes talks in her sleep.
examples.chinese: 凯蒂有时候睡觉说梦话。

examples.english: Grandad died peacefully in his sleep.
examples.chinese: 祖父是他睡着的时候安详地去世的。

examples.english: A sudden noise on the street woke Eileen from a deep sleep.
examples.chinese: 街上一个突然的声音把艾琳从沉睡中惊醒。

examples.english: It was nine o'clock and Nicky was still asleep.
examples.chinese: 9点了，尼基还在睡。

examples.english: We found mom asleep on the sofa.
examples.chinese: 我们发现妈妈在沙发上睡着了。

examples.english: Deborah, are you asleep?
examples.chinese: 德博拉，你睡着了吗？

examples.english: By the time her father had carried her up to bed, the child was sound asleep.
examples.chinese: 那孩子在父亲把她抱到床上时已经酣然入睡了。

examples.english: The baby had been fast asleep ever since we arrived.
examples.chinese: 我们到了之后，宝宝就沉沉地睡着了。

examples.english: The old man was half asleep and barely able to respond to the policeman's questions.
examples.chinese: 老人半醒半睡的，几乎不能回答警察的问题。

expressions.text: get some sleep/catch up on some sleep

examples.english: You must stop worrying and try to get some sleep.
examples.chinese: 你必须放下心来睡一会儿。

examples.english: I'll have to finish the job, but I can catch up on some sleep tomorrow night.
examples.chinese: 我得把工作干完，不过明天晚上可以补睡一会儿。

examples.english: You can have a kip in the car on the way.
examples.chinese: 路上你可以在车上睡一会儿。

examples.english: There's nothing worse than other people around when you're trying to get some kip.
examples.chinese: 你想睡觉的时候四周有人，没有比这更糟了。

examples.english: He had fallen into a deep slumber by the fire.
examples.chinese: 他在炉火边酣然入睡。

examples.english: The giants awoke from their enchanted slumber.
examples.chinese: 中了魔法的巨人从昏睡中醒来。

examples.english: Coleridge wrote the line ‘My cradled infant slumbers peacefully’ after the death of his son.
examples.chinese: 柯尔律治在儿子死后写下了“我的小宝贝安然入睡”这一句诗。

centers.english: to sleep for a short time

examples.english: I was having a nap by the fire one evening when I was woken up by the doorbell
examples.chinese: 有一天晚上我正在炉火边瞌睡，门铃响了，我就醒了过来。

examples.english: Try to take a nap in the afternoons if you're feeling tired.
examples.chinese: 如果你累了，下午就打个吨。

examples.english: The director always takes a nap around this time.
examples.chinese: 这个时候董事总要小睡一会。

examples.english: A short nap can be enough to make you feel more energetic in the afternoon.
examples.chinese: 小睡一会足以使你在下午的时候精神倍增。

examples.english: He left his mother dozing by the fire.
examples.chinese: 他留下他妈妈一个人在炉火边打盹。

examples.english: Geoff lay dozing gently in a sunlounger.
examples.chinese: 杰夫躺在折叠式日光椅里轻轻地打着嗑睡。

examples.english: Some people managed to sleep, but most of us just dozed fitfully.
examples.chinese: 有些人睡着了，但我们大部分人只是间歇地打了一会盹。

examples.english: She lapsed into a doze.
examples.chinese: 她打起了瞌睡。

examples.english: The baby was snoozing peacefully in her stroller, so we stopped to have a drink.
examples.chinese: 宝宝在婴儿车上安静地睡着了，所以我们就停下来喝了点东西。

examples.english: The study showed that if pilots on long-haul flights take a brief snooze in the cockpit, they're more alert for the landing.
examples.chinese: 调查显示，远途航班的飞行员如果在驾驶舱里小睡一下，降落的时候会更加警觉。

examples.english: I'll go home, snatch a couple of hours’ sleep and meet you at four.
examples.chinese: 我回家去赶紧睡几个小时，4点钟和你碰头。

examples.english: I grabbed a little sleep on the train, but it wasn't enough.
examples.chinese: 我在火车上偷空睡了一会，但还是不够。

examples.english: Are you tired? Why don't you have a sleep this afternoon?
examples.chinese: 你累吗？下午睡一觉吧。

examples.english: Are you two going to stop talking and go to sleep?
examples.chinese: 你们俩还不停止讲话去睡觉吗？

examples.english: I looked over at Dave, but he had gone to sleep.
examples.chinese: 我看了一眼戴夫，他已经睡着了。

examples.english: He lay on the sofa and pretended to go to sleep.
examples.chinese: 他躺在沙发上假装睡着了。

examples.english: If I wake up in the night, it takes me ages to go back to sleep.
examples.chinese: 我如果夜里醒来，就要很长时间才能再入睡。

examples.english: Dad always falls asleep in front of the TV after Sunday lunch.
examples.chinese: 星期天吃过午饭后，爸爸总是在电视机前睡着。

examples.english: Has Monica fallen asleep yet?
examples.chinese: 莫妮卡睡着了没有？

examples.english: I must have fallen asleep with the light on last night.
examples.chinese: 昨晚我肯定是开着灯睡着了。

examples.english: One in seven road accidents is caused by drivers falling asleep at the wheel.
examples.chinese: 道路交通事故有1/7是驾驶员开车时睡着造成的。

examples.english: Sorry, I must have dozed off for a few minutes.
examples.chinese: 对不起，我肯定是睡着了几分钟。

examples.english: I was just beginning to doze off when the telephone rang.
examples.chinese: 我正要睡着时电话铃响了。

examples.english: At around 12.30, she did eventually drop off for an hour or so.
examples.chinese: 12点30分左右，她终于睡着，睡了约一小时。

examples.english: She kept dropping off for a few minutes, before waking with a start.
examples.chinese: 她不停地打瞌睡，断断续续地睡几分钟，后来突然惊醒了。

examples.english: Janir had dropped off to sleep on the living room couch.
examples.chinese: 亚尼尔已经在客厅的长沙发上睡着了。

examples.english: Sarah had almost nodded off when Victor suddenly spoke.
examples.chinese: 萨拉刚要打瞌睡，维克托突然开口说话了。

examples.english: As the speaker droned on, only the occasional nudge from my husband kept me from nodding off.
examples.chinese: 发言人没完没了地在说着，我丈夫不时地轻轻推我一下，我才不至于睡着。

examples.english: He must have drifted off again, for when he awoke, the train had come to a halt.
examples.chinese: 他肯定又睡过去了，因为他醒来的时候，火车已经停下来了。

examples.english: She was just starting to drift off, when she heard a scream downstairs.
examples.chinese: 她迷迷糊糊正要入睡，突然听到楼下有尖叫声。

examples.english: That night as he drifted off to sleep, Quincy tried to imagine what the day would have been like if Marta had been there.
examples.chinese: 那天晚上，昆西想象着这天要是玛尔塔也在的话那会怎么样呢，想着想着就睡着了。

examples.english: Is the baby off yet?
examples.chinese: 宝宝睡着了没有？

examples.english: I always wait until he's off before I turn the light out.
examples.chinese: 我总是等他睡着了才关灯。

examples.english: I went back to bed, and was out like a light.
examples.chinese: 我回到床上，很快便睡着了。

examples.english: After a day on the ranch, you'll be out like a light, I can tell you.
examples.chinese: 在农场忙活一天，我保证你倒头就睡。

examples.english: ‘Did you get any sleep last night?’ ‘Yeah, I crashed out as soon as my head hit the pillow.’
examples.chinese: “昨天晚上你睡着过吗？”“我的头一碰到枕头就睡着了。”

examples.english: He'd flaked out on my bed.
examples.chinese: 他倒在我床上很快就睡着了。

centers.english: to get into your bed in order to sleep

examples.english: Do you want to go to bed, or watch the movie?
examples.chinese: 你想睡觉还是看这部电影？

examples.english: Mom, do I have to go to bed right now?
examples.chinese: 妈妈，我现在就要上床睡觉吗？

examples.english: She had planned to go to bed early that night, but a friend stopped by to see her.
examples.chinese: 那天晚上她本来是要早睡的，可是有一个朋友过来看她。

examples.english: I went to bed at nine last night, and I'm still tired.
examples.chinese: 昨天晚上我9点就上床了，但现在还是很累。

examples.english: Marianne took a shower and went straight to bed.
examples.chinese: 玛丽安娜洗了个澡就直接上床了。

examples.english: Sorry, were you in bed? I thought it might be too late to call you.
examples.chinese: 对不起，你已经上床了？我是想过，现在打电话给你可能太晚了。

examples.english: I'm usually in bed by 10.30 on weekdays.
examples.chinese: 平日我一般都是10点半之前上床睡觉。

examples.english: You were supposed to be in bed by now!
examples.chinese: 你现在应该上床睡觉了！

examples.english: It's late -- it must be nearly bedtime.
examples.chinese: 很晚了，肯定差不多是睡觉的时候了。

examples.english: Lucy, 7.30 is bedtime, you know that.
examples.chinese: 露西，7点半该上床了，你是知道的。

examples.english: This medicine should be taken at bedtime and first thing in the morning.
examples.chinese: 这种药应在睡前和起床后各服一次。

examples.english: Isn't it your bedtime?
examples.chinese: 你睡觉的时候到了吧？

examples.english: It's past my bedtime -- I really must get some sleep.
examples.chinese: 已经过了我该上床的时间了，我真的该睡一下了。

examples.english: Come on you guys, it's time to turn in.
examples.chinese: 各位，该睡觉了。

examples.english: I'm going to have to turn in. I'm not used to these late nights.
examples.chinese: 我得睡觉去了，我可不习惯天天熬夜。

examples.english: Usually I come home, eat dinner, watch a little TV, and then hit the sack by 9:30 or 10:00.
examples.chinese: 我通常回家，吃饭，看一会儿电视，然后在9点半或10点之前上床睡觉。

examples.english: I'm bushed. I think I'll hit the hay.
examples.chinese: 我累坏了，我想我要上床睡觉了。

examples.english: The captain retired at ten o'clock with a glass of whisky.
examples.chinese: 上尉在10点钟喝了一杯威士忌后就去睡了。

examples.english: Mary Ellen always had to set the fire for the next morning before retiring to bed.
examples.chinese: 玛丽·埃伦总是得先把第二天早上使用的炉火预备好，然后才上床睡觉。

centers.english: to sleep well and not wake up during the night

examples.english: ‘Did the storm keep you awake?’ ‘No, I slept very well.’
examples.chinese: “暴风雨把你吵醒了吗？”“没有，我睡得很好。”

examples.english: I don't want you to worry. Sleep well.
examples.chinese: 我不想让你担心，好好睡吧。

examples.english: The day had been long and difficult, but Gita slept soundly until morning.
examples.chinese: 那一天很长很不好过，不过吉塔还是一直酣睡到第二天早上。

examples.english: After getting a good night's sleep, Pedro awoke refreshed and full of energy.
examples.chinese: 佩德罗好好地睡了一个晚上，醒来时精神振作，充满活力。

examples.english: Unfortunately, fatigue cannot be cured just by having a good night's sleep.
examples.chinese: 可惜疲劳不是光靠一晚上睡得好就能恢复的。

examples.english: I think we both need a good night's sleep. I'm too tired to talk about it now.
examples.chinese: 我想我们俩都需要好好睡一个晚上。我太累了，现在不想谈这事。

examples.english: You'll feel better after you've had a good sleep.
examples.chinese: 好好睡一觉以后你会感觉好多了。

examples.english: We both had a good sleep on the plane, so the jetlag wasn't too bad for either of us.
examples.chinese: 在飞机上我们俩都好好地睡了一觉，所以时差反应不是太大。

examples.english: Cara slept like a log right through the storm.
examples.chinese: 这样的狂风暴雨，卡拉还是睡得很熟。

examples.english: It was deathly quiet, and I slept like a log all night.
examples.chinese: 四周一片死寂，我整晚都睡得很熟。

examples.english: His prison cellmate had slept through the tragedy.
examples.chinese: 悲剧发生的时候，与他同牢房的人一直都在睡觉。

examples.english: Can you imagine paying all that money to see an opera, and then sleeping through the whole thing?
examples.chinese: 你能想象吗？花了那么多钱去看歌剧，却从头到尾都在睡觉！

examples.english: I sometimes think Dave could sleep through a world war.
examples.chinese: 我有时候觉得，戴夫睡觉时世界大战都吵不醒他。

examples.english: The traffic won't bother me -- I'm a heavy sleeper.
examples.chinese: 往来的车辆吵不着我—我睡觉很沉。

examples.english: Normally, she was a good sleeper, but that night she lay awake, tossing and turning.
examples.chinese: 通常她睡得很沉，可那天晚上她躺在床上翻来覆去，怎么也睡不着。

examples.english: I'm sorry I didn't hear the phone -- I must have been dead to the world this morning.
examples.chinese: 对不起，我没听到电话响一今天早上我肯定睡得很死。

centers.english: to sleep badly or be unable to sleep

examples.english: I'm sorry, I didn't sleep very well last night and it's put me in a bad mood.
examples.chinese: 对不起，我昨晚没睡好，所以心情不好。

examples.english: They slept badly on the hard bamboo floor.
examples.chinese: 竹地板很硬，他们睡得不好。

examples.english: If you can't get to sleep, don't get up or have a meal or snack; relax and read quietly instead.
examples.chinese: 如果睡不着，就不要起来，也不要吃饭或吃零食，而是要放松下来，静静地阅读。

examples.english: I just couldn't get to sleep, what with all the traffic and people in the street.
examples.chinese: 街道上车水马龙，人声嘈杂，我根本睡不着觉。

examples.english: The people next door are having a party, so we probably won't get much sleep tonight.
examples.chinese: 隔壁人家在举行聚会，所以今天晚上我们可能会睡不好觉。

examples.english: She cried all last night and I didn't get much sleep either.
examples.chinese: 昨晚她哭了一整晚，我也没睡好。

examples.english: I'm a light sleeper - so I woke up as soon as I heard him come in.
examples.chinese: 我睡得不沉，所以一听到他进来我就醒了。

examples.english: I just hope your dad isn't a light sleeper.
examples.chinese: 我就希望你爸爸睡觉好一点。

examples.english: I was so worried, I didn't sleep a wink last night.
examples.chinese: 我担心得昨晚无法入睡。

examples.english: He had hardly slept a wink all night, beside himself with jealousy and anger.
examples.chinese: 他昨晚几乎一夜都没合眼，嫉妒和愤怒使他丧失了理智。

examples.english: I used to lie awake at night wondering what had happened to her.
examples.chinese: 夜里我经常会躺着无法入睡想知道她出了什么事情。

examples.english: I lay awake the whole night after I read the letter, thinking about what it could mean.
examples.chinese: 我看了信之后彻夜难眠，思索着这是什么意思。

examples.english: We'd lie awake, listening to our parents arguing in the room below.
examples.chinese: 我们躺在床上睡不着，听着父母在楼下的房间里争吵。

examples.english: She had slept badly, tossing and turning before falling into a fitful doze.
examples.chinese: 她睡得很不好，翻来覆去，后来好不容易时断时续地睡了一会。

examples.english: Do you fall asleep as soon as your head hits the pillow, or do you toss and turn for hours before dropping off?
examples.chinese: 你是头一靠着枕头就能入睡呢，还是要翻来覆去好几个小时才能睡着？

examples.english: After a sleepless night, she looked almost as pale and exhausted as Elinor.
examples.chinese: 一夜没睡，她看上去几乎和埃莉亚诺一样苍白憔悴。

examples.english: He's given us a few sleepless nights over the years, but we love him.
examples.chinese: 这些年来，他也让我们有过一些不眠之夜，但是我们爱他。

examples.english: Tom had spent a sleepless night on the sofa.
examples.chinese: 汤姆在沙发上一夜没睡。

examples.english: Another restless night followed, but she determinedly settled down to work again the next morning.
examples.chinese: 又一个不眠之夜，但是第二天早上她还是决意继续专心工作。

examples.english: I'd had quite a restless night, and breakfast didn't look appetizing.
examples.chinese: 我一夜没有安睡，早餐也显得毫无味道。

examples.english: Working outdoors all day certainly did wonders for my insomnia.
examples.chinese: 整天在户外干活无疑对我的失眠症产生了奇妙的作用。

examples.english: a cure for insomnia
examples.chinese: 失眠症治疗方法

examples.english: He suffered from insomnia and was taking sleeping pills each night.
examples.chinese: 他患了失眠症，每天晚上都服用安眠药。

examples.english: My mother was alarmed by my fits of weeping and chronic insomnia.
examples.chinese: 我间歇哭泣，而且长期失眠，使我母亲感到很不安。

examples.english: I'm an incurable insomniac so I get a lot of my work done while the world sleeps.
examples.chinese: 我是个无可救药的失眠症患者，所以我的许多工作都是在大家都睡觉的时候干的。

examples.english: His eyes were still red-rimmed from tears and sleeplessness.
examples.chinese: 他因为哭过，而且失眠，眼圈依然是红红的。

examples.english: Sleeplessness and loss of appetite are common signs of stress.
examples.chinese: 失眠和食欲不振是压力的常见征兆。

centers.english: to sleep for longer than usual

expressions.text: sleep in/ lie in/have a lie-in

examples.english: Where are Diane and Mike? Sleeping in again, huh?
examples.chinese: 黛安娜和迈克在哪里？又在睡懒觉啊？

examples.english: There's not much chance of a lie-in when you've got three kids.
examples.chinese: 你要是有三个孩子，就不可能睡懒觉了。

examples.english: We had a lie-in and breakfast in bed.
examples.chinese: 我们睡了个懒觉，在床上吃了早餐。

examples.english: In the end I decided the best thing was to put you to bed and let you sleep it off.
examples.chinese: 最后我决定，最好的办法是让你上床睡一觉，那就没事了。

examples.english: Martin's still in bed, sleeping off his hangover.
examples.chinese: 马丁还在床上靠睡觉消除他的醉意。

examples.english: He was taken to the local hospital and kept in overnight to sleep off the effects of the drug.
examples.chinese: 他被送往当地的医院住了一晚，睡一觉以消除药品的作用。

centers.english: to sleep in a place where you do not usually sleep
centers.chinese: 睡在一个不常睡的地方

examples.english: Are your friends sleeping over tonight?
examples.chinese: 你的朋友们今晚要在这里过夜吗?

examples.english: Is it okay if I sleep over at Sam's house tomorrow night?
examples.chinese: 我明天晚上到萨姆家睡行吗？

examples.english: You can crash out at my place if you like.
examples.chinese: 你如果喜欢，可以在我这儿过夜。

examples.english: Would you mind if I crashed on your couch?
examples.chinese: 我在你的沙发上睡可以吗？

examples.english: The party finished late, so I just dossed on the floor at Adele's.
examples.chinese: 晚会很晚才结束，所以我就在阿黛尔家的地板上睡。

examples.english: You can stay here, if you don't mind dossing down on the floor.
examples.chinese: 你如果不介意睡地板的话，可以留在这里过夜。

examples.english: What I like most about camping is sleeping out.
examples.chinese: 对于野营，我最喜欢的就是露天睡觉。

examples.english: Over 100 people will be sleeping out in Glasgow tonight to highlight the plight of the homeless.
examples.chinese: 今晚格拉斯哥将有超过100人露宿街头，以突显无家可归者的困境。

examples.english: Bring a sleeping bag with you, because we're going to sleep out and walk back tomorrow.
examples.chinese: 你带一个睡袋，因为我们要睡在户外，明天再走回来。

examples.english: Hundreds of homeless people have to sleep rough every night in London.
examples.chinese: 伦敦每天晚上都有数以百计的无家可归者要露宿街头。

examples.english: The number of teenagers sleeping rough on the streets is on the increase.
examples.chinese: 露宿街头的青少年人数有增无减。

examples.english: I was forced to sleep rough that night in a disused warehouse.
examples.chinese: 那晚我不得不在一个废弃的仓库里随便找个地方睡。

centers.english: to make someone go to sleep

examples.english: Certain types of music always send me to sleep.
examples.chinese: 某些类型的音乐总能使我入睡。

examples.english: ‘Drink this,’ mother said, ‘It'll send you to sleep.’
examples.chinese: “把这个喝了，”母亲说道，“很快你就能睡着。”

examples.english: The sound of her rhythmic breathing finally put me to sleep, and we both slept until the sun rose.
examples.chinese: 她有节奏的呼吸终于让我也睡着了，我们俩都一直睡到太阳升起。

examples.english: It's sometimes very difficult to get my young son off to sleep when he's excited.
examples.chinese: 我那小儿子兴奋的时候有时很难哄他入睡。

examples.english: She cried for a while but I finally got her off to sleep.
examples.chinese: 她哭了一会，不过我最后还是把她哄睡了。

examples.english: Usually, I put the kids to bed at about 8:00.
examples.chinese: 通常我是在8点钟左右把孩子们哄上床。

examples.english: In those days, many children were put to bed before dark in the summer months.
examples.chinese: 那时夏天的几个月里，很多小孩子都是天黑以前就被打发上床的。

centers.english: to not go to bed and not sleep

examples.english: You guys go ahead and go to bed. I think I'll stay up for a while.
examples.chinese: 你们睡觉去，我想我要晚点才睡。

examples.english: Kate stayed up all night by his bedside.
examples.chinese: 凯特整夜都没睡，守在他的床边。

examples.english: Didn't you even stay up on election night?
examples.chinese: 你们大选之夜都没有熬夜？

examples.english: We went to bed, but Julie and Kate stayed up talking and playing cards.
examples.chinese: 我们去睡了，但朱莉和凯特整夜在谈天、玩纸牌。

expressions.text: stay up till the small/wee hours

examples.english: That night Carl stayed up into the small hours, preparing work for the next day.
examples.chinese: 那天晚上卡尔熬夜到凌晨，准备第二天的工作。

examples.english: Julie's parents waited up all night but she never came home.
examples.chinese: 朱莉的父母整夜都在等她，但她一直没有回家。

examples.english: I'll be home late tonight, so don't wait up.
examples.chinese: 我今晚很晚才会回来，所以别等我了。

examples.english: I should go - Marie will be waiting up for me.
examples.chinese: 我要走了，玛丽会等着我不睡的。

examples.english: John, are you awake? I think I heard someone downstairs.
examples.chinese: 约翰，你醒着吗？我好像听到楼下有人。

examples.english: When she returned to the bedroom, Jamie was awake.
examples.chinese: 她回到卧室时，杰米醒了。

examples.english: I was still wide awake at 2:00 a.m. when Jody came home.
examples.chinese: 零晨两点钟乔迪回家的时候我还是醒着，毫无睡意。

examples.english: Gretchen wandered into the kitchen, only half awake and looking for coffee.
examples.chinese: 格蕾琴还没有完全醒过来，迷迷糊糊地走进厨房去找咖啡。

examples.english: Ellen was determined to stay awake, despite the late hour.
examples.chinese: 埃伦决意不让自己睡着，不管有多晚。

examples.english: I was jolted awake by a blaring car horn outside.
examples.chinese: 我被外面响亮的汽车喇叭声猛然惊醒了。

examples.english: The next morning, Benjamin shook me awake.
examples.chinese: 第二天早晨，本杰明把我摇醒。

examples.english: Molly kept Paula awake all night talking.
examples.chinese: 莫莉整个晚上说个没完，让葆拉无法睡。

examples.english: Angry neighbours say they are regularly kept awake by guests leaving the hotel late at night.
examples.chinese: 愤怒的邻居说，夜里很晚离开酒店的客人经常把他们吵醒。

examples.english: These terrifying thoughts sometimes kept me awake for hours.
examples.chinese: 这些可怕的想法有时候让我几个小时不得入睡。

examples.english: Arnold would keep us all up with his long, rambling stories.
examples.chinese: 阿诺德总是讲一些东拉西扯的长篇故事，让我们无法睡觉。

examples.english: I'm often kept up by the noise of laughter and music from next door.
examples.chinese: 我经常被隔壁的笑声和音乐声吵得不能入睡。

centers.english: someone who likes to be awake at night

examples.english: I've become a bit of a night owl since I started living alone.
examples.chinese: 我自从开始一个人居住之后，就变成了一个夜猫子。

examples.english: I never remember my dreams when I wake up.
examples.chinese: 我醒来时从来都记不住做过什么梦。

examples.english: When she woke, she found that it was all a dream.
examples.chinese: 她醒来时发现不过是南柯一梦。

examples.english: None of my dreams are about work.
examples.chinese: 我做的梦都和工作无关。

examples.english: I had a strange dream last night -- you and I were in some sort of tropical forest.
examples.chinese: 我昨晚做了个奇怪的梦—我和你在一个热带森林里。

examples.english: The events of the past few days seemed like a bad dream.
examples.chinese: 前几天发生的事恰似一场噩梦。

examples.english: When I was younger, I had recurring dreams in which I was constantly pursued by soldiers.
examples.chinese: 小时候我反复做到的梦是被一帮士兵紧追不舍。

examples.english: He woke from a nightmare, trembling with fear.
examples.chinese: 他从梦魇中惊醒，吓得浑身颤栗。

examples.english: Years after the accident I still have nightmares about it.
examples.chinese: 这场事故过去了那么多年，我还是做噩梦。

examples.english: Ingrid was brought out of her daydream by a shout from her mother.
examples.chinese: 母亲的一声叫喊把英格丽德从白日梦中唤醒过来。

examples.english: Neil seemed lost in a daydream, and didn't hear what I said.
examples.chinese: 尼尔似乎沉浸在白日梦中，我说的话他根本没听见。

examples.english: The doorbell rang, shaking me from my reverie.
examples.chinese: 门铃响了，把我从美妙幻想里拉回到现实。

examples.english: Sometimes he would drift off into reverie, and gaze out of the window for hours.
examples.chinese: 有时候他会浸沉于幻想之中，一连几个小时凝视着窗外。

examples.english: He had a dream in which he was running through the forest, being chased by a bear.
examples.chinese: 他做了一个梦，梦中他在森林里奔跑，一头熊在后面追赶着他。

examples.english: I keep having the same dream about trying to get across a deep river.
examples.chinese: 我老是做着同一个梦，想过一条水很深的河。

labels: past tense and past participle: British/American

examples.english: Do animals dream?
examples.chinese: 动物做梦吗？

examples.english: I hoped that someone would wake me up, that I had only been dreaming.
examples.chinese: 我希望有人叫醒我，我只是在做梦。

examples.english: I dreamt about you last night.
examples.chinese: 昨晚我梦到你了。

examples.english: Stephanie often dreams of long sea journeys.
examples.chinese: 斯蒂芬妮经常梦见自己在海上长途旅行。

examples.english: I dreamed that I was lying on a beach in the Caribbean.
examples.chinese: 我梦见自己躺在加勒比海的沙滩上。

examples.english: Last night I dreamt I went to Manderley again.
examples.chinese: 昨晚我梦见自己又回到了曼德利。

examples.english: At school, he was always being told to ‘stop daydreaming’.
examples.chinese: 在学校里老是有人叫他“别做白日梦”。

examples.english: Colin began to daydream about what he would do if he won the lottery.
examples.chinese: 科林开始做起了白日梦，幻想要是他赢了彩票，他要做些什么。

examples.english: She woke early the next morning, and slipped out of the house unseen.
examples.chinese: 第二天早上，她一早醒来，偷偷溜出了房子。

examples.english: Babies often wake because they are hungry.
examples.chinese: 婴儿醒来常常是因为饿了。

examples.english: The dog suddenly woke up and started barking.
examples.chinese: 那条狗突然惊醒，狂吠起来。

expressions.text: wake up at 5 a.m./12 noon etc

examples.english: I woke up at five o'clock and couldn't get back to sleep again.
examples.chinese: 我5点钟醒来，无法再入睡。

examples.english: ‘Are you awake, Lucy?’ she whispered.
examples.chinese: “你醒了吗，露茜？”她轻声问。

examples.english: I'm usually awake before anyone else.
examples.chinese: 我一般比别人都醒得早。

examples.english: It was nearly three in the morning, but Jill was still wide awake.
examples.chinese: 差不多凌晨3点钟了，吉尔还毫无睡意。

examples.english: He listened, only half awake, as the teacher's voice droned on.
examples.chinese: 老师的声音絮絮不休，他半睡半醒地听着。

examples.english: Barely awake, we stumbled out of the tent to find ourselves in a foot of water.
examples.chinese: 我们半睡半醒，跌跌撞撞走出帐篷，发现自己站在一英尺深的水中。

examples.english: I've stopped drinking coffee in the evenings, as it tends to keep me awake at night.
examples.chinese: 我晚上不喝咖啡了，喝了夜里总是睡不着。

examples.english: Some members of the audience were clearly having difficulty staying awake.
examples.chinese: 有些观众显然难以保持清醒。

examples.english: Ben lay awake, worrying about next day's exam.
examples.chinese: 本担心第二天的考试，睡不着。

examples.english: I've lain awake at nights, turning the problem over and over in my mind.
examples.chinese: 我晚上睡不着，脑子里翻来覆去想着这个问题。

examples.english: She was coming round after her operation, but she still felt dizzy and very sleepy.
examples.chinese: 手术后，她苏醒过来，但仍感觉头晕嗜睡。

examples.english: Henry's eyelids flickered. ‘He's coming around!’ Marie cried.
examples.chinese: 亨利的眼皮动了动。“他醒了！”玛丽喊道。

examples.english: As I entered the room, she stirred slightly, then went back to sleep.
examples.chinese: 我进房时，她微微动了一下，然后又睡了。

examples.english: Roger momentarily stirred, turned in the bed and murmured something inaudible.
examples.chinese: 罗杰在床上动了动，翻了个身，又咕哝了一句听不清楚的话。

examples.english: Be quiet or you'll wake my parents.
examples.chinese: 安静点，你要吵醒我父母了。

examples.english: We were woken by a loud banging on the door.
examples.chinese: 我们被重重的敲门声吵醒。

examples.english: He shook her arm to try and wake her.
examples.chinese: 他摇晃她的胳膊，想把她叫醒。

examples.english: The alarm clock woke me up at 8 o'clock.
examples.chinese: 闹钟在8点钟把我唤醒。

examples.english: Why didn't you wake me up this morning? I was late for work.
examples.chinese: 今天早上你为什么不把我叫醒？我上班迟到了。

examples.english: They were making enough noise to wake up the whole street!
examples.chinese: 他们吵得把整条街的人都弄醒了！

examples.english: Come on honey, wake up! You'll be late!
examples.chinese: 快点，亲爱的，醒醒！你要迟到了！

examples.english: I got undressed in the bathroom to avoid disturbing her.
examples.chinese: 我在浴室里脱衣服，以免吵醒她。

examples.english: I hope my snoring won't disturb you too much.
examples.chinese: 我希望我打鼾不会老是吵醒你。

examples.english: a disturbed night's sleep
examples.chinese: 睡得不安稳的一夜

examples.english: He found Paula fast asleep in bed, and nothing would rouse her.
examples.chinese: 他发现葆拉在床上睡得很熟，怎么也叫不醒。

examples.english: I was roused from my sleep by the sound of a door banging shut.
examples.chinese: 砰的关门声把我从梦中惊醒。

expressions.text: get somebody up/get somebody out of bed

examples.english: Go and get your brother up. It's time for him to go to work.
examples.chinese: 去叫你哥哥起床，他该上班去了。

examples.english: Did you get me up just to tell me that?
examples.chinese: 你把我吵醒就是为了告诉我这事？

examples.english: I'm sorry for calling so early - I hope I didn't get you out of bed.
examples.chinese: 对不起那么早打电话给你—希望没把你吵醒。

examples.english: What time do you need to get up tomorrow?
examples.chinese: 明天你得什么时候起床?

examples.english: Why is it always me who gets up first?
examples.chinese: 为什么老是我先起床？

examples.english: Frank gets up at half past five every morning.
examples.chinese: 弗兰克每天早上5点半起床。

examples.english: I think we should get up early and leave before breakfast.
examples.chinese: 我想我们应该早点起床，早餐前出发。

examples.english: She goes to bed late and gets up late.
examples.chinese: 她睡得晚，起得也晚。

examples.english: I couldn't face getting out of bed this morning.
examples.chinese: 今天早上我真不愿意起床。

examples.english: Isn't it about time you got out of bed?
examples.chinese: 现在该是你起床的时候了吧？

examples.english: Is Harry up yet?
examples.chinese: 哈里起来了吗？

examples.english: I was up at six this morning.
examples.chinese: 今天早上我6点钟起来。

examples.english: Jake had been up since dawn.
examples.chinese: 杰克天刚亮就起来了。

examples.english: You're up early!
examples.chinese: 你起得早啊！

examples.english: ‘Have you seen Cathy?’ ‘No, she hasn't surfaced yet.’
examples.chinese: “你见到凯茜了吗？”“没有，她还没起床呢。”

centers.english: to stay in bed until late in the morning

examples.english: We usually get up late on Sundays.
examples.chinese: 星期天我们一般起得晚。

examples.english: Jackson's not here. He must have gotten up late again.
examples.chinese: 杰克逊不在，他肯定又起晚了。

examples.english: I got up late, and then Brian was in the bathroom, so I just rushed straight here.
examples.chinese: 我起晚了，布赖恩又在用卫生间，所以我就直接奔到这里来了。

examples.english: It's Saturday tomorrow, so I can have a lovely long lie in.
examples.chinese: 明天是星期六，我可以好好睡个懒觉。

examples.english: We slept late, and when we woke the sun was blazing in through the windows.
examples.chinese: 我们睡了个懒觉，醒来时，耀眼的阳光从窗外直射进来。

examples.english: If you're not well, you'd better stay in bed.
examples.chinese: 你要是不舒服的话，最好待在床上。

examples.english: We stayed in bed all morning, reading the papers and drinking coffee.
examples.chinese: 我们整个上午都在床上，看看报纸、喝喝咖啡。

examples.english: Sorry I'm late - I overslept.
examples.chinese: 对不起，我迟到了—我睡过头了。

examples.english: They were afraid of oversleeping and missing the plane.
examples.chinese: 他们担心睡过头，误了飞机。

examples.english: He had overslept on the day he was supposed to take the entrance exam.
examples.chinese: 入学考试那天他睡过了头。

examples.english: I think I'll go to bed late tonight and sleep in tomorrow.
examples.chinese: 我想今晚我会晚点睡，明天晚点起床。

examples.english: She doesn't even like sleeping in, even on Sundays.
examples.chinese: 她甚至不喜欢睡懒觉，即使星期天也一样。

centers.english: someone who is easily woken by noise

examples.english: Mr and Mrs Carlton are both light sleepers, so be very quiet when you come in at night.
examples.chinese: 卡尔顿先生和太太睡觉时都很容易惊醒，你晚上回来时动作要非常轻。

examples.english: If you are a light sleeper, ask for a room that doesn't face the street.
examples.chinese: 如果你是个容易被吵醒的人，就要一间不朝大街的房间。

centers.english: someone who usually gets up early in the morning

examples.english: He'd been an early riser ever since he was a child.
examples.chinese: 他从小就是个早起的人。

examples.english: A few early risers were already sipping their cappuccinos in the bars facing the square.
examples.chinese: 一些早起的人已经在广场对面的酒吧里喝卡布奇诺咖啡了。

examples.english: It took four strong men to lift the piano.
examples.chinese: 要四个强壮男子才抬得动这架钢琴。

examples.english: Unless you have very strong arms, get a workman to do the drilling for you.
examples.chinese: 叫一个工人来帮你钻吧，除非你手臂很有力气。

examples.english: Sarah's big and strong, just like the men she works with.
examples.chinese: 萨拉长得又大又壮，就和她的男同事一样。

examples.english: Twenty years of working in the steel mill had made him as strong as an ox.
examples.chinese: 在钢铁厂工作了20年使他练就了牛一般强壮的身体。

examples.english: He was a tall man with a powerful physique.
examples.chinese: 他是一个体格强壮的高个子男人。

examples.english: She liked men who were tall and muscular.
examples.chinese: 她喜欢身材高大、体格健壮的男人。

examples.english: He had broad shoulders and muscular arms.
examples.chinese: 他有宽阔的肩膀，肌肉发达的手臂。

examples.english: He kept his firm muscular body in shape with an hour's run every morning.
examples.chinese: 他每天晨跑一小时，使身体保持结实强壮。

examples.english: A well-built young man in uniform came to our table and asked the time.
